hooks.text: You paid your supplier 20,000 dollars by PayPal. The receipt says 20,000. You actually spent closer to 22,000. Both numbers are real.
PayPal feels fine on a 200 dollar payment. On a 20,000 dollar supplier payment, three different charges stack up that the app never adds together for you.
Send your factory 20,000 dollars on PayPal. The transaction fee takes 880. The exchange rate quietly takes 700 more. That is before the supplier markup.
A business owner thought PayPal was the safe way to pay his China supplier. Then he lined up the fee, the rate, and the quote, and the number stopped looking safe.
Every 20,000 dollar order you push through PayPal is bleeding around 1,500 dollars in fees and rate, plus a price uplift the supplier baked into your invoice.
